easy_001_question: How many times do Muslims pray every day?
easy_001_explanation: Muslims pray 5 times daily: Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha.
easy_002_question: What is the holy book of Islam called?
easy_002_explanation: The Quran is the holy book revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
easy_003_question: What is the first month of the Islamic calendar?
easy_003_explanation: Muharram is the first month of the Islamic (Hijri) calendar.
easy_004_question: What do we say before eating?
easy_004_explanation: We say Bismillah (In the name of Allah) before eating.
easy_005_question: What is the name of the month when Muslims fast?
easy_005_explanation: Ramadan is the blessed month of fasting.
easy_006_question: What is the holy city where Muslims go for Hajj?
easy_006_explanation: Mecca is the holiest city in Islam where the Kaaba is located.
easy_007_question: Who was the last Prophet in Islam?
easy_007_explanation: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) is the last and final messenger of Allah.
easy_008_question: What do we say after sneezing?
easy_008_explanation: We say Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah) after sneezing.
easy_009_question: How many pillars of Islam are there?
easy_009_explanation: There are 5 pillars: Shahada, Salah, Zakat, Sawm, and Hajj.
easy_010_question: What is the cube-shaped building in Mecca called?
easy_010_explanation: The Kaaba is the sacred house of Allah in Mecca.
easy_011_explanation: Assalamu Alaikum means "Peace be upon you".
easy_012_question: What is the name of the angel who brought revelations to prophets?
easy_012_explanation: Angel Jibreel (Gabriel) delivered Allah's messages to prophets.
easy_013_explanation: Eid ul-Fitr celebrates the end of Ramadan fasting.
easy_014_question: What direction do Muslims face when praying?
easy_014_explanation: Muslims face the Kaaba in Mecca when praying.
easy_015_question: What is the Arabic word for God?
easy_015_explanation: Allah is the Arabic word for God.
easy_016_question: What is the first prayer of the day called?
easy_016_explanation: Fajr is the dawn prayer, the first of the five daily prayers.
easy_017_question: What do we say when we hear good news?
easy_017_explanation: MashaAllah means "What Allah has willed" - said for good things.
easy_018_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) and his son Ismail (AS) built the Kaaba.
easy_019_question: What is charity called in Islam?
easy_019_explanation: Zakat is obligatory charity, one of the five pillars of Islam.
easy_020_question: What is the night prayer called?
easy_020_explanation: Isha is the night prayer, the last of the five daily prayers.
easy_021_question: What animal did Prophet Yunus (AS) stay inside?
easy_021_explanation: Prophet Yunus (Jonah) was swallowed by a whale.
easy_022_question: What is the place of worship for Muslims called?
easy_022_explanation: Masjid (Mosque) is where Muslims gather to pray.
easy_023_question: What do we say when starting something new?
easy_023_explanation: Bismillah means "In the name of Allah".
easy_024_question: How many chapters (Surahs) are in the Quran?
easy_025_question: What is the first Surah of the Quran?
easy_025_explanation: Al-Fatiha (The Opening) is the first Surah of the Quran.
easy_026_question: What do we say to thank someone in Islam?
easy_026_explanation: JazakAllah Khair means "May Allah reward you with goodness".
easy_027_question: What is fasting called in Arabic?
easy_027_explanation: Sawm means fasting, especially during Ramadan.
easy_028_question: What is the sunset prayer called?
easy_028_explanation: Maghrib is the prayer performed just after sunset.
easy_029_question: Who was the father of Prophet Ismail (AS)?
easy_029_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) was the father of Ismail (AS).
easy_030_question: What do we say when we see something beautiful?
easy_031_question: What is the declaration of faith called?
easy_031_explanation: Shahada is the declaration that there is no god but Allah.
easy_032_question: What city did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) migrate to?
easy_032_explanation: The Prophet migrated from Mecca to Medina (Hijra).
easy_033_question: What is the afternoon prayer called?
easy_034_question: What do we say when someone dies?
easy_034_option_1: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi rajiun
easy_034_explanation: This means "We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return".
easy_035_question: What is the pilgrimage to Mecca called?
easy_035_explanation: Hajj is the annual pilgrimage to Mecca, a pillar of Islam.
easy_036_question: What color is often associated with Islam?
easy_036_explanation: Green is traditionally associated with Islam and paradise.
easy_037_question: What is the midday prayer called?
easy_038_question: What do we say to ask for forgiveness?
easy_038_explanation: Astaghfirullah means "I seek forgiveness from Allah".
easy_039_question: What is the special night in Ramadan called?
easy_039_explanation: Laylatul Qadr (Night of Power) is better than 1000 months.
easy_040_question: What animal is mentioned in the story of Prophet Salih (AS)?
easy_040_explanation: Allah sent a she-camel as a miracle for Prophet Salih's people.
easy_041_question: What is the Friday prayer called?
easy_041_explanation: Jumu'ah is the congregational Friday prayer.
easy_042_question: What do we say when we wake up?
easy_042_explanation: We thank Allah (Alhamdulillah) for waking us up.
easy_043_question: Who was known as the "Friend of Allah"?
easy_043_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) is called Khalilullah (Friend of Allah).
easy_044_question: What is the call to prayer called?
easy_044_explanation: Adhan is the call to prayer announced from the mosque.
easy_045_question: What do we say when entering the bathroom?
easy_045_explanation: We seek Allah's protection from evil when entering the bathroom.
easy_046_question: What is the last Surah of the Quran?
easy_046_explanation: An-Nas (Mankind) is the 114th and last Surah.
easy_047_question: What is the Eid celebrated during Hajj called?
easy_047_explanation: Eid ul-Adha (Festival of Sacrifice) is during Hajj.
easy_048_question: What do we say when leaving the house?
easy_048_explanation: We say "In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah".
easy_049_question: What is the prayer mat called?
easy_049_explanation: Musalla is the prayer mat or rug used for praying.
easy_050_question: What do we say when finishing a meal?
easy_050_explanation: We say Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah) after eating.
medium_001_question: How many years did it take for the Quran to be revealed?
medium_001_explanation: The Quran was revealed over 23 years to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
medium_002_question: What is the name of Prophet Muhammad's (PBUH) first wife?
medium_002_explanation: Khadijah (RA) was the first wife and first believer.
medium_003_question: In which cave did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) receive the first revelation?
medium_003_explanation: The first revelation came in Cave Hira on Mount Nur.
medium_004_question: How many fardh rakats are in Fajr prayer?
medium_005_question: What is the name of the well near the Kaaba?
medium_005_explanation: Zamzam is the blessed well that appeared for Hajar and Ismail.
medium_006_question: Who was the first Muezzin (caller to prayer) in Islam?
medium_006_explanation: Bilal ibn Rabah (RA) was the first Muezzin.
medium_007_explanation: Subhanallah means "Glory be to Allah" or "Allah is perfect".
medium_008_question: How many times do we walk between Safa and Marwa during Hajj?
medium_008_explanation: Sa'i involves walking 7 times between Safa and Marwa.
medium_009_question: What is the name of the Prophet who could talk to animals?
medium_009_explanation: Prophet Sulaiman (Solomon) could understand animals and jinn.
medium_010_question: What is the Arabic term for the direction of prayer?
medium_010_explanation: Qibla is the direction Muslims face when praying (towards Kaaba).
medium_011_explanation: Prophet Nuh (Noah) built the Ark before the great flood.
medium_012_question: What is the name of the night journey of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
medium_012_explanation: Isra and Miraj was the miraculous night journey to Jerusalem and heavens.
medium_013_question: How many names of Allah are mentioned in Islamic tradition?
medium_013_explanation: There are 99 beautiful names of Allah (Asma ul-Husna).
medium_014_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was thrown into fire?
medium_014_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) was thrown into fire but Allah made it cool.
medium_015_explanation: Taqwa means being conscious of Allah and avoiding sins.
medium_016_question: Which Surah is known as the "Heart of the Quran"?
medium_016_explanation: Surah Yasin is called the heart of the Quran.
medium_017_question: What is the name of Prophet Yusuf's (AS) father?
medium_017_explanation: Prophet Yaqub (Jacob) was the father of Yusuf (Joseph).
medium_018_question: How many fardh rakats are in Maghrib prayer?
medium_019_question: What is the first word revealed in the Quran?
medium_019_explanation: Iqra (Read/Recite) was the first word revealed.
medium_020_question: What is the name of the Prophet who parted the sea?
medium_020_explanation: Prophet Musa (Moses) parted the Red Sea with Allah's permission.
medium_021_explanation: Sadaqah is voluntary charity, while Zakat is obligatory.
medium_022_question: Who was the first Caliph after Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
medium_022_explanation: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq (RA) was the first Caliph.
medium_023_question: What is the name of the special prayers during Ramadan nights?
medium_023_explanation: Taraweeh prayers are performed during Ramadan nights.
medium_024_question: Which Prophet was given the Zabur (Psalms)?
medium_024_explanation: Prophet Dawud (David) was given the Zabur.
medium_025_explanation: Ihsan means worshipping Allah as if you see Him.
medium_026_question: How many brothers did Prophet Yusuf (AS) have?
medium_027_question: What is the name of the mountain where the Quran was first revealed?
medium_027_explanation: Mount Nur (Light) is where Cave Hira is located.
medium_028_question: What is the Arabic word for "patience"?
medium_029_question: Which Prophet is known as "Kalimullah" (One who spoke to Allah)?
medium_029_explanation: Prophet Musa (AS) spoke directly to Allah.
medium_030_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was raised to heaven alive?
medium_030_explanation: Prophet Isa (Jesus) was raised to heaven by Allah.
medium_031_explanation: Dua means supplication or calling upon Allah.
medium_032_question: How many times do we circle the Kaaba during Tawaf?
medium_032_explanation: Tawaf involves circling the Kaaba 7 times.
medium_033_question: What is the name of the Prophet known for his beautiful voice?
medium_033_explanation: Prophet Dawud (AS) had a beautiful voice for reciting Zabur.
medium_034_explanation: Iman means faith or belief in Allah and His messengers.
medium_035_question: Which Surah mentions the story of the People of the Cave?
medium_035_explanation: Surah Al-Kahf (The Cave) tells the story of the sleepers.
medium_036_question: What is the name of Prophet Muhammad's (PBUH) daughter?
medium_036_explanation: Fatimah (RA) was the beloved daughter of the Prophet.
medium_037_explanation: Tawbah means repentance and turning back to Allah.
medium_038_question: How many fardh rakats are in Dhuhr prayer?
medium_039_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was known for his patience?
medium_039_explanation: Prophet Ayyub (Job) is famous for his patience during trials.
medium_040_explanation: Shukr means gratitude and thankfulness to Allah.
medium_041_question: Which Prophet was given the Injeel (Gospel)?
medium_041_explanation: Prophet Isa (Jesus) was given the Injeel.
medium_042_question: What is the name of the uncle who raised Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
medium_042_explanation: Abu Talib raised and protected the Prophet.
medium_043_explanation: Tawakkul means placing complete trust in Allah.
medium_044_question: How many Prophets are mentioned by name in the Quran?
medium_044_explanation: 25 Prophets are mentioned by name in the Quran.
medium_045_question: What is the name of the Prophet who interpreted dreams?
medium_045_explanation: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) was gifted with dream interpretation.
medium_047_question: Which battle was the first major battle in Islam?
medium_047_explanation: The Battle of Badr was the first major battle.
medium_048_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was swallowed by a whale?
medium_048_explanation: Prophet Yunus (Jonah) was swallowed by a whale.
medium_049_explanation: Barakah means blessing and divine grace from Allah.
medium_050_question: How many fardh rakats are in Isha prayer?
hard_001_question: In which year of Prophethood did the Hijra (migration) to Medina occur?
hard_001_explanation: The Hijra occurred in the 13th year of Prophethood (622 CE).
hard_002_question: What is the name of the treaty signed between Muslims and Quraysh?
hard_002_explanation: The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was signed in 6 AH.
hard_003_question: How many verses (Ayat) are in Surah Al-Baqarah?
hard_003_explanation: Surah Al-Baqarah has 286 verses, the longest Surah.
hard_004_question: What is the name of the angel responsible for rain?
hard_004_explanation: Angel Mikail is responsible for rain and sustenance.
hard_005_question: In which Surah is Ayatul Kursi found?
hard_005_explanation: Ayatul Kursi is verse 255 of Surah Al-Baqarah.
hard_006_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given control over wind?
hard_006_explanation: Prophet Sulaiman (AS) was given control over wind and jinn.
hard_007_question: What is the meaning of "Ulul Azm"?
hard_007_explanation: Ulul Azm refers to the five greatest Prophets of strong will.
hard_008_question: How many years did Prophet Nuh (AS) call his people to Islam?
hard_009_question: What is the name of the mountain where Prophet Musa (AS) received the Torah?
hard_009_explanation: Prophet Musa (AS) received the Torah on Mount Sinai (Tur).
hard_010_question: What is the name of the companion who compiled the Quran into one book?
hard_010_explanation: Zaid ibn Thabit (RA) compiled the Quran under Abu Bakr's order.
hard_011_explanation: Tawheed is the belief in the absolute oneness of Allah.
hard_012_question: Which Surah is called "The Mother of the Quran"?
hard_012_explanation: Al-Fatiha is called Umm al-Quran (Mother of the Quran).
hard_013_question: What is the name of the angel who will blow the trumpet on the Day of Judgment?
hard_013_explanation: Angel Israfil will blow the trumpet (Sur) on Judgment Day.
hard_014_question: How many times is the name "Muhammad" mentioned in the Quran?
hard_014_explanation: The name Muhammad is mentioned 4 times in the Quran.
hard_015_question: What is the name of the first mosque built in Islam?
hard_015_explanation: Masjid Quba was the first mosque built by the Prophet.
hard_016_explanation: Shirk is the sin of associating partners with Allah.
hard_017_question: Which Prophet is mentioned most in the Quran?
hard_017_explanation: Prophet Musa (AS) is mentioned 136 times in the Quran.
hard_018_question: What is the name of the year when Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was born?
hard_018_explanation: The Prophet was born in the Year of the Elephant (570 CE).
hard_019_explanation: Sunnah refers to the teachings and practices of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
hard_020_question: How many Juz (parts) is the Quran divided into?
hard_020_explanation: The Quran is divided into 30 Juz (parts).
hard_021_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was known as "Ruhullah" (Spirit of Allah)?
hard_022_question: What is the meaning of "Hadith Qudsi"?
hard_022_explanation: Hadith Qudsi are words of Allah narrated by the Prophet.
hard_023_question: Which Surah does not begin with "Bismillah"?
hard_023_explanation: Surah At-Tawbah (9) is the only Surah without Bismillah.

hard_024_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) made birds from clay by Allah's permission.
hard_025_explanation: Ijma is the consensus of Islamic scholars on a matter.
hard_026_question: How many times is "Bismillah" mentioned in the Quran?
hard_026_explanation: Bismillah appears 114 times (113 at Surah starts + once in An-Naml).
hard_027_question: What is the name of the wife of Pharaoh who believed in Allah?
hard_027_explanation: Asiya, wife of Pharaoh, is one of the best women in Islam.
hard_028_explanation: Qiyas is analogical reasoning in Islamic jurisprudence.
hard_029_question: Which companion is known as "The Sword of Allah"?
hard_029_explanation: Khalid ibn al-Walid (RA) was called Saifullah (Sword of Allah).
hard_030_question: What is the name of the longest verse in the Quran?
hard_030_explanation: Ayat ad-Dayn (2:282) about debt is the longest verse.
hard_031_explanation: Fiqh is Islamic jurisprudence - understanding of Islamic law.
hard_032_question: How many daughters did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) have?
hard_032_explanation: The Prophet had 4 daughters: Zainab, Ruqayyah, Umm Kulthum, and Fatimah.
hard_033_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was known as "Safiyyullah" (Chosen by Allah)?
hard_034_explanation: Ijtihad is independent reasoning by qualified scholars.
hard_035_question: Which Surah is equivalent to one-third of the Quran?
hard_035_explanation: Surah Al-Ikhlas is equivalent to one-third of the Quran.
hard_036_question: What is the name of the first woman to accept Islam?
hard_036_explanation: Khadijah (RA) was the first woman to accept Islam.
hard_037_explanation: Wudu is the ritual ablution performed before prayer.
hard_038_question: How many times is the word "Salah" (prayer) mentioned in the Quran?
hard_038_explanation: The word Salah appears approximately 67 times in the Quran.
hard_039_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was known as "Dhun-Nun" (Man of the Whale)?
hard_040_explanation: Tayammum is dry ablution using clean earth when water is unavailable.
hard_041_question: Which companion was known as "Al-Farooq" (The Distinguisher)?
hard_041_explanation: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was called Al-Farooq.
hard_042_question: What is the name of the night when the Quran was first revealed?
hard_042_explanation: Laylatul Qadr (Night of Power) is when the Quran was first revealed.
hard_043_explanation: Khushu is humility and deep concentration during prayer.
hard_044_question: How many sons did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) have?
hard_044_explanation: The Prophet had 3 sons: Qasim, Abdullah, and Ibrahim.

hard_046_explanation: Riba is interest or usury, which is prohibited in Islam.

hard_047_explanation: Surah Yusuf (Chapter 12) tells his complete story.

hard_048_explanation: Sumayya bint Khayyat (RA) was the first martyr in Islam.
hard_049_explanation: Istikhara is a prayer seeking Allah's guidance in decisions.
hard_050_question: How many times is the name "Isa" (Jesus) mentioned in the Quran?
hard_050_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) is mentioned 25 times in the Quran.
easy_051_question: What is the name of the Prophet who had a staff that turned into a snake?
easy_051_explanation: Prophet Musa's (AS) staff turned into a snake as a miracle from Allah.

easy_052_explanation: We say "Sallallahu Alaihi Wasallam" (Peace be upon him) after his name.

easy_053_explanation: Allah rewards good deeds with Jannah (Paradise).

easy_054_explanation: This means "There is no power except with Allah".

easy_055_explanation: Malak al-Mawt (Angel of Death) takes souls when people die.

easy_056_explanation: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) was known for his exceptional beauty.

easy_057_explanation: Zamzam water is blessed water from the well near the Kaaba.

easy_058_explanation: InshaAllah means "If Allah wills" - said for future plans.

easy_059_explanation: Maryam (Mary) was the blessed mother of Prophet Isa (Jesus).

easy_060_explanation: The Day of Judgment (Yawm al-Qiyamah) is when Allah will judge everyone.

easy_061_explanation: Hajar al-Aswad (Black Stone) is in the corner of the Kaaba.

easy_062_explanation: Prophet Nuh (Noah) lived for over 950 years.

easy_063_explanation: SubhanAllah means "Glory be to Allah" - said when amazed.

easy_064_explanation: Ihram is the white garment worn during Hajj and Umrah.

easy_065_explanation: Prophet Adam (AS) was the first human created by Allah.

easy_066_explanation: Hawwa (Eve) was the first woman and wife of Adam (AS).

easy_067_explanation: Jannah (Paradise) is the reward for believers.

easy_068_explanation: Surah Al-Ikhlas teaches about the Oneness of Allah.

easy_069_explanation: This means "We belong to Allah and to Him we return".

easy_070_explanation: Prophet Sulaiman (Solomon) was both a Prophet and a king.

medium_051_explanation: Prophet Yusuf (AS) was thrown into a well by his jealous brothers.

medium_053_explanation: Al-Fatiha is recited once in each rakat of prayer.

medium_054_explanation: Prophet Musa (Moses) was given the Torah (Tawrat).
medium_055_explanation: Rizq means sustenance or provision from Allah.

medium_056_explanation: Abu Bakr (RA) was called As-Siddiq (The Truthful One).
medium_057_explanation: Dhikr means remembrance of Allah through words and heart.

medium_061_explanation: Standing at Mount Arafat is the most important part of Hajj.

medium_070_explanation: Prophet Hud (AS) was sent to the people of Ad.

hard_051_explanation: Prophet Salih (AS) was sent to the people of Thamud.

hard_053_explanation: The word "Quran" is mentioned approximately 70 times.

hard_054_explanation: Prophet Shuaib (AS) was sent to the people of Madyan.
hard_055_explanation: Tajweed is the set of rules for proper Quran recitation.
hard_056_question: Which Surah is named after a woman?
hard_056_explanation: Surah Maryam is named after Maryam (Mary), mother of Isa (AS).

hard_058_explanation: Battle of Khandaq (Trench) - Muslims dug a trench to defend Medina.
hard_059_explanation: Muttaqeen refers to the pious, God-fearing believers.
hard_060_question: How many Surahs in the Quran begin with "Alif Lam Meem"?
hard_060_explanation: 6 Surahs begin with Alif Lam Meem: Baqarah, Al-Imran, Ankabut, Rum, Luqman, Sajdah.

hard_061_explanation: The four Caliphs were among the ten promised Paradise (Ashara Mubashara).

hard_066_explanation: Ummah refers to the global Muslim community.
hard_067_question: Which Surah protects from the punishment of the grave?

hard_068_explanation: Sirat al-Mustaqeem means the Straight Path to Allah.
hard_069_question: What is the name of the bridge over Hellfire?
hard_069_explanation: Sirat is the bridge over Hellfire that everyone must cross.
hard_070_explanation: Mizan is the Scale on which deeds will be weighed on Judgment Day.
easy_071_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was born without a father?
easy_071_explanation: Prophet Isa (Jesus) was born miraculously without a father.
easy_072_question: What do we say when we yawn?
easy_072_option_0: Cover mouth and seek refuge from Shaytan
easy_072_explanation: We cover our mouth and seek refuge from Shaytan when yawning.
easy_073_question: What is the name of the evil being who disobeyed Allah?
easy_073_explanation: Shaytan (Iblis) disobeyed Allah and was expelled from Paradise.
easy_074_question: What is the name of the tree in Jannah (Paradise)?
easy_074_explanation: Sidrat al-Muntaha is a special tree in the highest Paradise.
easy_075_question: What do we say before sleeping?
easy_075_explanation: We say "O Allah, in Your name I die and I live" before sleeping.
easy_076_question: What is the name of the Prophet who had a coat of many colors?
easy_076_explanation: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) was given a special coat by his father.
easy_077_question: What is the name of the place where bad people go after death?
easy_077_explanation: Jahannam (Hell) is the punishment for disbelievers.
easy_078_question: What do we say when entering a mosque?
easy_078_explanation: We ask Allah to open the doors of His mercy when entering a mosque.
easy_079_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was swallowed by a big fish?
easy_079_explanation: Prophet Yunus (Jonah) was swallowed by a whale.
easy_080_question: What do we say when leaving a mosque?
easy_080_explanation: We ask Allah for His bounty when leaving a mosque.
easy_081_question: What is the name of the Prophet who split the moon?
easy_081_explanation: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) split the moon as a miracle.
easy_082_question: What is the name of the month before Ramadan?
easy_082_explanation: Shaban is the month before Ramadan.
easy_083_question: What do we say when we see a new moon?
easy_083_explanation: We make a special dua when seeing the new moon.
easy_084_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was a shepherd?
easy_084_explanation: All Prophets were shepherds at some point in their lives.
easy_085_question: What is the name of the month after Ramadan?
easy_085_explanation: Shawwal is the month after Ramadan, when Eid ul-Fitr is celebrated.
easy_086_question: What do we say when drinking water?
easy_086_explanation: We say Bismillah before drinking water.
easy_087_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was thrown into prison?
easy_087_explanation: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) was imprisoned in Egypt.
easy_088_question: What do we say after drinking water?
easy_088_explanation: We say Alhamdulillah after drinking water.
easy_089_question: What is the name of the bird mentioned in the story of Prophet Ibrahim (AS)?
easy_089_explanation: Allah showed Ibrahim how He brings the dead to life using four birds.
easy_090_question: What do we say when we hear thunder?
easy_090_explanation: We glorify Allah and say "SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi" when we hear thunder.
easy_091_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was saved from the fire?
easy_091_explanation: Allah made the fire cool and safe for Prophet Ibrahim (AS).
easy_092_question: What do we say when we see rain?
easy_092_explanation: We ask Allah for beneficial rain.
easy_093_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given a miracle table from heaven?
easy_093_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) asked Allah for a table spread with food from heaven.
easy_094_question: What do we say when we look in the mirror?
easy_094_option_1: Allahumma ahsanta khalqi fa ahsin khuluqi
easy_094_explanation: We ask Allah to beautify our character as He beautified our appearance.
easy_095_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given iron-working skills?
easy_095_explanation: Prophet Dawud (AS) was given the ability to soften iron.
easy_096_question: What do we say when wearing new clothes?
easy_096_explanation: We thank Allah for providing us with new clothes.
easy_097_question: What is the name of the Prophet who could heal the sick?
easy_097_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) could heal the sick by Allah's permission.
easy_098_question: What do we say when visiting someone who is sick?
easy_098_explanation: We say "No worry, it is a purification, if Allah wills".
easy_099_question: What is the name of the Prophet who could bring the dead back to life?
easy_099_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) could bring the dead back to life by Allah's permission.
easy_100_question: What do we say when we complete something successfully?
easy_100_explanation: We say Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah) when we complete something.
medium_071_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was sent to the people of Sodom?
medium_071_explanation: Prophet Lut (Lot) was sent to the people of Sodom.
medium_072_explanation: Qiyam means standing, as in standing in prayer.
medium_073_question: What is the name of the companion known as "The Lion of Allah"?
medium_073_explanation: Hamza ibn Abdul-Muttalib (RA) was called Asadullah (Lion of Allah).
medium_074_explanation: Witr is an odd-numbered prayer prayed after Isha.
medium_075_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the Scrolls (Suhuf)?
medium_075_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) was given the Scrolls (Suhuf).
medium_076_explanation: Tashahhud is the testimony of faith recited while sitting in prayer.
medium_077_question: What is the name of the companion who was the first child to accept Islam?
medium_077_explanation: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was the first child to accept Islam.
medium_078_explanation: Salam means peace, as in Assalamu Alaikum (Peace be upon you).
medium_079_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was known for his strength?
medium_079_explanation: Prophet Dawud (David) was known for his great strength.
medium_080_explanation: Takbir is saying "Allahu Akbar" (Allah is the Greatest).
medium_081_question: What is the name of the companion who was the first freed slave to accept Islam?
medium_081_explanation: Bilal ibn Rabah (RA) was among the first freed slaves to accept Islam.
medium_082_explanation: Tasbih is saying "SubhanAllah" (Glory be to Allah).
medium_083_question: What is the name of the Prophet who defeated Goliath?
medium_084_explanation: Tahmid is saying "Alhamdulillah" (Praise be to Allah).
medium_085_question: What is the name of the companion who was known for his knowledge?
medium_085_explanation: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) was known as the scholar of the Ummah.
medium_086_explanation: Duha is the forenoon time, when the optional Duha prayer is prayed.
medium_087_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the miracle of the staff?
medium_087_explanation: Prophet Musa's (AS) staff could turn into a snake and part the sea.
medium_088_explanation: Janazah is the funeral prayer performed for the deceased.
medium_089_question: What is the name of the companion who narrated the most hadith?
medium_089_explanation: Abu Hurairah (RA) narrated the most hadith.
medium_090_question: What is the meaning of "Sujud as-Sahw"?
medium_090_explanation: Sujud as-Sahw is the prostration of forgetfulness in prayer.
medium_091_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the miracle of the glowing hand?
medium_091_explanation: Prophet Musa's (AS) hand would glow white as a miracle.
medium_092_question: What is the meaning of "Sujud at-Tilawah"?
medium_092_explanation: Sujud at-Tilawah is prostration when reciting certain Quran verses.
medium_093_question: What is the name of the companion who was known for his bravery?
medium_093_explanation: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was known for his exceptional bravery.
medium_094_question: What is the meaning of "Sujud ash-Shukr"?
medium_094_explanation: Sujud ash-Shukr is the prostration of gratitude to Allah.
medium_095_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the miracle of speaking as a baby?
medium_095_explanation: Prophet Isa (AS) spoke as a baby to defend his mother.
medium_096_explanation: Khutbah is the speech given during Friday and Eid prayers.
medium_097_question: What is the name of the companion who was known for his modesty?
medium_097_explanation: Uthman ibn Affan (RA) was known for his exceptional modesty.
medium_098_explanation: Imam is the leader who leads the congregational prayer.
medium_099_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given control over the jinn?
medium_099_explanation: Prophet Sulaiman (AS) was given control over the jinn.
medium_100_explanation: Ma'mum is the follower who prays behind the Imam.
hard_071_question: What is the name of the angel who guards Hellfire?
hard_071_explanation: Malik is the angel who guards Hellfire.
hard_072_explanation: Qadr means divine decree or predestination by Allah.
hard_073_question: How many gates does Jannah (Paradise) have?
hard_074_explanation: Barzakh is the barrier or intermediate life between death and resurrection.
hard_075_question: How many gates does Jahannam (Hellfire) have?
hard_076_question: What is the meaning of "Hawd al-Kawthar"?
hard_076_explanation: Hawd al-Kawthar is the Pool of the Prophet on the Day of Judgment.
hard_077_question: What is the name of the two angels who question in the grave?
hard_077_explanation: Munkar and Nakir are the angels who question in the grave.
hard_078_explanation: Shafa'ah means intercession, especially by the Prophet on Judgment Day.
hard_079_question: What is the name of the two angels who record our deeds?
hard_079_explanation: Raqib and Atid are the angels who record our good and bad deeds.
hard_080_explanation: Fitrah is the natural disposition or innate nature to believe in Allah.
hard_081_explanation: Surah Al-Mulk means "The Sovereignty" or "The Kingdom".
hard_082_explanation: Kufr means disbelief or rejection of faith.
hard_083_question: How many levels of Jannah are there?
hard_083_explanation: There are 100 levels in Jannah, with Firdaus being the highest.
hard_084_explanation: Nifaq means hypocrisy - showing faith while hiding disbelief.
hard_085_question: What is the name of the highest level of Paradise?
hard_085_explanation: Firdaus is the highest level of Paradise.
hard_086_question: What is the meaning of "Maqasid ash-Shariah"?
hard_086_explanation: Maqasid ash-Shariah means the objectives or purposes of Islamic law.
hard_087_question: Which Surah mentions the story of the Elephant?
hard_087_explanation: Surah Al-Fil (The Elephant) tells the story of Abraha's army.
hard_088_question: What is the meaning of "Usul al-Fiqh"?
hard_088_explanation: Usul al-Fiqh means the principles or foundations of Islamic jurisprudence.
hard_089_question: What is the name of the angel who guards Paradise?
hard_089_explanation: Ridwan is the angel who guards Paradise.
hard_090_explanation: Istidraj is when Allah gradually leads sinners to destruction through blessings.
hard_092_explanation: Tafakkur means reflection or contemplation on Allah's creation.
hard_093_question: How many Surahs are named after Prophets?
hard_093_explanation: 6 Surahs are named after Prophets: Yunus, Hud, Yusuf, Ibrahim, Muhammad, Nuh.
hard_094_explanation: Tadabbur means deep reflection and pondering over the Quran.
hard_095_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the title "Khalilullah"?
hard_095_explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) was called Khalilullah (Friend of Allah).
hard_096_explanation: Wara means piety or scrupulousness in avoiding doubtful matters.
hard_097_explanation: Surah Al-Furqan means "The Criterion" (between right and wrong).
hard_098_explanation: Zuhd means asceticism or detachment from worldly possessions.
hard_099_question: What is the name of the Prophet who was given the title "Kaleemullah"?
hard_099_explanation: Prophet Musa (AS) was called Kaleemullah (One who spoke to Allah).
hard_100_explanation: Muraqabah means self-vigilance or watchfulness over one's actions.
